mnemonics: PAWS: Probability of an event, Affecting Weighted Sum
RAIN: Randomly Identified Events happen Normally
keyPoints: Definition: Probability is a number between 0 and 1 that represents the likelihood of an event occurring
Theoretical Probability: calculated as the number of favorable outcomes divided by the total number of outcomes
Experimental Probability: calculated as the number of favorable outcomes divided by the total number of trials
Independent Events: probability of two events occurring together is the product of their individual probabilities
Mutually Exclusive Events: probability of two events occurring together is the sum of their individual probabilities
